rejected_candidates.title: Swaps and share minting have no on-chain slippage bounds or deadlines
rejected_candidates.reason: These entrypoints return the achieved output/share amount, so wrappers can enforce min-out or min-share checks atomically by reverting after the call; this is not a standalone core-protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan repayment validation uses OR instead of AND
rejected_candidates.reason: The initial `||` check is followed by per-side deficit checks that require any shortfall on one token to be economically covered by surplus of the other token; simply repaying one side and stealing the other does not pass.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in pricing calculations when reserves are depleted
rejected_candidates.reason: Some pricing paths do revert in zero-sided states, but the submitted claim does not establish a permissionless permanent lockup or fund-loss path; the pool can still be rebalanced through liquidity operations.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked token transfer return values in flash loan callback
rejected_candidates.reason: Outgoing token transfers use `SafeERC20.safeTransfer`, and the callback itself does not transfer tokens; the alleged exploit depends on the rejected flash-loan-underpayment claim.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee rate model owner can set arbitrary fees to extract value
rejected_candidates.reason: This describes explicit owner/governance authority over the fee model rather than an unintended vulnerability in the pool logic.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection on flash loan repayment
rejected_candidates.reason: `flashLoan()` does not execute an external market sale on behalf of the pool; borrowers either return assets or leave surplus collateral, so the slippage framing is incorrect.

rejected_candidates.title: Permit function lacks deadline protection against front-running
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing a permit to be used at exactly its deadline timestamp via `deadline >= block.timestamp` matches standard permit semantics and is not a reportable vulnerability.
